steps.ordered: CT Abdomen (CTA ABD W&W/O C & RECONS)

ensemble.other_hypothesis: Active lower GI hemorrhage from diverticular source (hepatic flexure/right colon) vs. colonic AVM/angiodysplasia; the CTA is being ordered to localize the bleeding source and characterize active extravasation prior to potential angiographic or surgical intervention
ensemble.information_gap: The history references a CTA performed at the transferring facility showing high-density material near the hepatic flexure suggestive of active extravasation off an SMA branch, but that outside imaging is not yet available or formally read at this institution; the physician needs cross-sectional vascular imaging in-house to confirm the bleeding source location, characterize the feeding vessel, and assess whether bleeding has resumed or stopped before deciding on colonoscopy vs. IR embolization vs. surgery
ensemble.expected_finding: Active contrast extravasation or a blush in the right colon/hepatic flexure region supplied by an SMA branch, with associated diverticula, confirming the source and guiding targeted intervention
ensemble.appropriateness_reason: The patient has a massive, hemodynamically significant lower GI bleed with an outside report of active extravasation near the hepatic flexure, and obtaining a formal in-house CTA with arterial and venous phases is the appropriate next step to confirm the bleeding source, assess for ongoing hemorrhage, and guide definitive intervention (IR embolization or surgery).
grounding: History: 'CTA performed which showed high density material around hepatic flexure, suggestive of active extravasation off branch of SMA'
History: '1L of blood was suctioned from and around rectum'
History: 'her SBP dropped to the ___ soon after arrival'
History: 'She received 1U PRBC, with initial HCT on arrival of 31.7'
Imaging already resulted BEFORE this decision point: '(none — only the baseline workup below was available)'
ensemble.reasoning: This elderly woman with known diverticulosis presented with massive hematochezia causing hemodynamic instability, and the outside facility's CTA suggested active extravasation from an SMA branch near the hepatic flexure — but that imaging is not yet formally available at this institution. Ordering an in-house CTA abdomen with and without contrast and reconstructions allows me to independently confirm the bleeding source, determine whether extravasation is ongoing now that she has temporarily stabilized, and provide the precise vascular roadmap needed for IR embolization or surgical planning. Without this study in hand, I cannot safely commit to colonoscopy, angiography, or the operating room.

verification.actual_finding: A focal area of amorphous hyperdensity within the lumen of the ascending colon proximal to the hepatic flexure is identified, concerning for active extravasation into the bowel lumen, in the setting of diffuse colonic diverticula.
verification.relation_to_expectation: The in-house CTA directly confirms the expected finding of active contrast extravasation in the right colon/hepatic flexure region with associated diverticula, consistent with the outside facility's report and localizing the bleeding source to an SMA-territory branch as anticipated.
